entry.title: Terminal Fun
entry.content:
My plan is to ban the mouse out of my daily use System’s and be productive too. In this Post i present some tools that can help me do that.

Daily work, so what do i have to tweak? The Web Browser is startet with the login of course and then there is the Text Editing that has to be taken care of. Music and other tools have to be managed. One still can’t throw away the graphical power browsers but i found some tools to take care of the first steps into mouseless using your computer.

As i reviewed in one of my Posts. Take this list as a first time contact.

My Tools

  • urxvt (extendable terminal)
    • solarized-theme (best eye friendly theme ever)
    • mpd (Music Player Daemon)
      • ncmpcpp (gui for mpd)
    • tmux (Terminal Multiplexer aka the better screen)
    • ranger (file manager for the console)
    • zsh (programmable and fully customizable shell)
      • oh-my-zsh (script collection for zsh)
  • firefox
    • vimperator (vim like bindings for firefox)
      • scripting the web
    • duckduckgoplus (search-engine alternative)
    • noscript (block the tracking scripts and annoying popups)
    • adblock plus (block the spam)
  • vim (very hard in the beginning but it’s worth it)
    • jekyll.vim (jekyll blog edit help)
    • solarized (solarized theme)
    • tagbar
    • vim-colors-solarized
    • vim-fugitive
    • vim-misc
    • vim-notes (Write Notes into your Dropbox)

Puppet in the end

for more easy configuration i developed a puppet script that installs all that for you. HOW TO PUPPET LINK In the future there will be an dotfiles repository from me for the coolest settings by far for the most of the tools.

a little configuration

DOTFILES REPO FOR THE MASSES

entry.title: Silverlight Videos on Linux
entry.content:
Silverlight and linux are not friends.

This is the Tutorial from which i could watch movies for silverlight successfully on my Ubuntu Machine. Pipelight Installation

So for Ubuntu you have to do some little things … For my personal recommends i added chromium to split the usage of a normal browser and a dedicated one.

bash sudo add-apt-repository ppa:pipelight/stable sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install --install-recommends pipelight-multi sudo pipelight-plugin --enable silverlight sudo apt-get install chromium-browser

go out there and Happy Silverlighting…

entry.title: IPv6 Private Adresses in Linux
entry.content:
To activate the private Adress room in Ubuntu you have to add the following line to your /etc/sysctl.conf:

net.ipv6.conf.all.use_tempaddr=2

!!!!!!!!!!!!! Funktioniert noch nicht so richtig!neinein! …

entry.title: TMUX - TerminalMUltipleXer
entry.content:
TMUX. Another great invention. Tmux is like screen but better. TerminalMUltipleXer it’s called and that’s what it does. With this little program you are able to log into a Remote system and let sessions open for later use. For Screen you have to extensively tweak it to do your belongings and tmux just has it built in. My killer feature for this app is it’s ability to split Terminal Windows just like a tiling window manager does. Let’s say you have 200 ssh sessions open each in one seperate window. Now search through the windows with screen:
ctrl-a is it here? nope. ctrl-a is it here? nope ctrl-a
you get it.
Search for a specific text in all terminal windows in tmux:
ctrl-b f myservernumber1 and boom!
you got a list of all windows with that specific string in it.
I still not discovered all features but it’s insane what it does to your ssh and console behavoir.

Go and try it out.

Ubuntu:
apt-get install tmux

entry.title: Music in Linux
entry.content:
What is the best way to push your music through your home?
I tested many things like a centralized PC with xbmc and multicasting over the local network for nearly synchronized playback from many devices but this had an issue. The Devices that would go over WLAN had a serious lag and it was not practical because you couldn't use the wlan for anything more with the multicast hammering through the air.

After a few tries with php software and streaming server's i now have the perfect setup for me.
You know MPD? (Music Player Deamon!)
This nifty little program is so light that it also can be run on a pentium 2 or raspberry pi with not much of ram in use.

For the ones that use 2-4 client's to catch the music there is the option to let mpd build its own mp3 stream. But if you fire it to an icecast or shoutcast server this little one can bring the sound to the masses. If you want to know more contect me and let me know.
